action.content:
You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

1 remote, 1 box, 1 frame, 1 cup, 1 flower

Your secret value function is:

remote: 4, box: 4, frame: 1, cup: 0, flower: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content:
Message 1: Hi there! It seems we have a diverse set of items to divide. I propose we start by discussing our preferences. I value the remote and box the most, followed by the frame and flower. The cup has the least value to me.

Message 2: Given our limited communication, let's try to find a fair split. How about I take the remote and box, and you can have the frame and flower? We can discuss the cup later.

Message 3: I understand the importance of reaching an agreement quickly. If the above proposal doesn't work for you, I'm open to alternatives. Perhaps you'd prefer the remote, and I can take the box, frame, and flower?

Message 4: Let's try to find a compromise. If you value the frame highly, I can part with it. So, my new proposal is: I take the remote, box, and cup, while you get the frame and flower.

Message 5: This is my final offer before making a secret proposal. I believe this division is fair based on our values. If you agree, great! If not, my secret proposal will be: [Proposal: 1 remote, 1 box, 1 cup]. I hope we can reach a mutually beneficial agreement.
action.label: response

Let us start.

Your secret value function is:

remote: 3, box: 0, frame: 0, cup: 6, flower: 1

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
action.label: context

action.content:
Based on my value function and the available items, I propose the following distribution:

Proposal: 1 cup, 1 flower

This proposal aligns with my higher valuation of the cup and still leaves the remote and box for the other player, who seems to value them more based on their initial proposal. By doing so, we can both receive items we value highly, maximizing our collective score.
action.label: response

action.content: player was instructed to make a proposal
